content.content: East: Chaudandigadhi Municipality Ward No. 4, Basaha
West: Chaudandigadhi Municipality Ward No. 6, Satdhare
North: Chaudandigadhi Municipality Ward No. 8, Birendra Bazar
South: Chaudandigadhi Municipality Ward No. 6, Madan Bhandari Highway

content: Boundaries:

content.content: Upon evaluating the peace and security situation within the district, it has been decided to recommend that the Chief District Officer issue a curfew order for the following additional areas, effective from 07:00 PM tonight until 05:00 AM tomorrow morning.

content: Belaka Municipality Area:

content.content: East: Belaka Municipality Ward No. 9, Kharkhola
West: Belaka Municipality Ward No. 4, Lalbazar
North: Belaka Municipality Ward No. 9, Bhachuwa Water Tank
South: Belaka Municipality Ward No. 3, Gideri Khola
Decided to request all political parties, civil society, intellectuals, journalists, and stakeholders to help maintain peace, security, and order in the district.
Decided to mobilize the Nepalese Army, Nepal Police, and Armed Police Force to maintain security arrangements in the district.
Decided to manage security through the District Security Command Post and the JOC based on a micro-evaluation of the overall security situation.

content: District Administration Office, Okhaldhunga
Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: Discussions were held regarding potential security challenges arising from 'Gen-Z' protests currently taking place in Kathmandu and most districts against the government regarding the social media ban, corruption, and other issues. Given the possibility of similar protests in this district, security agencies shall coordinate to develop a security plan and ensure reliable arrangements to keep demonstrations peaceful and prevent any untoward incidents.
In view of the upcoming major festivals such as Dashain, Tihar, and Chhath, all three security agencies shall prepare and effectively implement an integrated festival-centric security action plan.
The District Police Office and the APF Dependent Gulm shall increase regular daily patrolling from all their respective units within the district.

A strategic sectoral security plan, in accordance with the Integrated District Security Plan prepared by the District Security Committee for the current fiscal year, shall be prepared in coordination with all security agencies and presented at the next meeting.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: The meeting discussed the nationwide Gen Z protests, their impact on Okhaldhunga district, and future security strategies. The security situation thus far has been found to be normal.

Expressed heartfelt tributes to those who lost their lives during protests in Kathmandu and other parts of the country since yesterday, extended deep condolences to the bereaved families, and wished for the speedy recovery of the injured.

Information has been received that protests may occur in this district in solidarity with the Gen Z protests that began yesterday (2082/05/23). Security agencies shall prioritize the safety of citizens' lives and property, with special focus on securing public assets (government offices, vehicles, local levels, residences of high-ranking officials, sensitive physical structures, and petrol pumps).

To normalize the impact of Gen Z protests and prevent untoward incidents, coordination shall be maintained with district political party leaders/representatives, civil society, local intellectuals, distinguished personalities, and agitating parties. Security agencies shall exercise full patience.
A sincere appeal is made to all district residents to keep demonstrations peaceful and disciplined, prevent self-interested infiltration, and protect public property.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25

A curfew order is issued under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, effective from 5:00 PM on 2082/05/25 until 6:00 AM on 2082/05/26. Movement, assembly, processions, demonstrations, meetings, or picketing are prohibited within the following areas of Okhaldhunga district.
Boundaries:

content.content: East: All areas of Rumjatar, Siddicharan Municipality Ward No. 4 and 5
West: All areas of Rampur (Molung Rural Municipality Ward No. 1 and 2) and Siddicharan Municipality Ward No. 9, 11, and 12
North: All areas of Siddicharan Municipality Ward No. 6 and 7
South: All areas of Siddicharan Municipality Ward No. 3, 8, and 10

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25 (Military Assistance)

content.content: Analyzing the critical situation arising from the protests on 2082/05/23, including arson, vandalism, and looting of property, as well as incidents of prisoners escaping from various district jails, there is a high probability of similar incidents in this district. As it may be difficult for the Nepal Police and Armed Police Force alone to control the situation and prevent criminal acts like theft and looting, it is decided to request additional assistance from the Nepalese Army (Durgabax Battalion) under Section 6(2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.
The peace and security situation of the district thus far was found to be normal.

content: Madhesh Province
District Administration Office, Bara
Decisions of 2082/05/22

content.content: Reviewed the implementation status of decisions made by the Security Committee up to August 29, 2082 (Bhadra 13).

Based on the letter from the District Police Office, Bara dated 2082/05/18, Simara Airport security is currently provided by 15 personnel under a Sub-Inspector. Considering the increase in physical structures and passenger traffic, and to meet international security standards, the existing personnel are deemed insufficient. It is decided to direct the District Police Office to provide an additional 12 personnel.
The current peace and security situation of the district was felt to be normal.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: Reviewed the implementation status of decisions made by the Security Committee up to September 7, 2082 (Bhadra 22).

Due to attempted attacks on government property, officials, and private assets during Gen Z protests, a curfew order is issued per Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. Movement and assembly are prohibited within the following boundaries of Kalaiya Sub-Metropolitan City and designated areas of Jitpursimara Sub-Metropolitan City from 2:00 PM to 8:00 PM today (2082/05/24).

content: Boundaries:

content.content: East: Pasaha River
West: Gandak Canal
North: Badaharwa Chowk
South: Up to Durga Secondary School
Up to 200 meters on both sides of the highway along the Gandak Canal to Pathlaiya road section.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24 (Night)

content.content: Reviewed implementation of decisions made on the morning of 2082/05/24.

As attacks on government and private property are possible, a district-wide curfew is issued from 10:00 PM tonight (2082/05/24) to 6:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/25) under Sections 6(a) and 6b(i). Due to difficulties in controlling the situation, it is decided to request military mobilization from the Ripumardan Battalion, Barahmpuri Barracks.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25

content.content: Reviewed implementation of decisions made up to 2082/05/24.
A district-wide curfew is again issued from 6:00 AM today (2082/05/25) to 6:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/26) under Section 6(a) and (b) to prevent attacks on property and personnel during protests.

Considering the critical national situation, it is decided to form a Joint Operation Center (JOC) led by the Chief District Officer and including all security heads to deploy personnel in an integrated manner.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/26

content.content: Reviewed implementation of decisions made up to 2082/05/25.
Decided to immediately designate prohibited areas and deploy integrated security personnel if any threat arises.
Decided to arrest and prosecute anyone taking advantage of the situation to cause damage, loot, or conduct violent attacks. Nightly curfews will be issued for several days as needed.
Decided to issue further notices based on security evaluations.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/26 (Evening)

content.content: Reviewed decisions from the morning of 2082/05/26.
A district-wide curfew is issued from 8:00 PM tonight (2082/05/26) to 6:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/27) to prevent looting, arson, and violent attacks.
Decided to issue further notices based on security evaluations.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/27

content.content: Reviewed implementation of decisions made at 2:30 PM on 2082/05/26.
A prohibitory order (Nishedhagya) is issued for Kalaiya Sub-Metropolitan City from 11:00 AM to 6:00 PM today (2082/05/27) under Section 6(3a).

A district-wide curfew is issued from 8:00 PM tonight (2082/05/27) to 6:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/28) under Sections 6(a) and 6b(i). Military assistance from the Ripumardan Battalion is requested due to the difficulty in maintaining control.
Decided to issue further notices based on security evaluations.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/28

content.content: Reviewed implementation of decisions made at 6:00 AM on 2082/05/28.
Since the abnormal situation resulting from protests has normalized, it is decided to lift all curfews and prohibitory orders after 6:00 AM on 2082/05/29.
Decided to issue further notices based on security evaluations.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/28 (Dissolution of House)

content.content: Reviewed implementation of decisions made at 10:00 AM on 2082/05/27.

Following the dissolution of the House of Representatives by the President on Friday, 2082/05/27, and considering potential illegal assemblies or violence, a prohibitory order is issued for the entire district from 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM today (2082/05/28). Additionally, a district-wide curfew is issued from 8:00 PM tonight to 6:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/29). Military assistance from the Ripumardan Battalion is requested per Section 6(2) and 6b(i).
Decided to issue further notices based on security evaluations.

content: District Administration Office, Parsa
Decisions of 2082/05/23

Based on security sensitivity analysis, a curfew order is issued under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, effective from 9:00 PM tonight (2082/05/23) to 9:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/24) within the following designated areas of Birgunj Metropolitan City. Movement and assembly are prohibited.
Boundaries:

content.content: East: Bypass Road
West: Sirsiya Bridge
North: Gandak Chowk
South: Shankaracharya Gate

content: Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: Reviewed the implementation status of previous District Security Committee decisions. The overall peace and security situation of the district was analyzed as normal.

In light of recent developments, district security agencies shall analyze security sensitivities, exchange information coordinately, and ensure effective and error-free management of the district's overall peace and security.

Decided to instruct government offices in the district to mandate guard rotations, restrict the use of government vehicles to essential business only, and ensure the protection of public property and office premises.

Reliable security arrangements shall be made at markets, public entities, political party offices, residences of VIPs, and public installations, considering security sensitivities. Nepal Police and Armed Police Force Nepal shall conduct joint and coordinated patrols and security deployments.

In case of crowds where damage to public property or risk to the lives of security personnel/individuals is likely, security forces shall first attempt persuasion. If unsuccessful, crowd control shall be managed progressively through baton charges, water cannons, and warning shots. If the crowd remains uncontained, a systematic (sequential) use of force shall be applied.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: A curfew was previously issued under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, within certain areas of Birgunj Metropolitan City, Parsa district, from 9:00 PM on 2082/05/23 to 9:00 AM on 2082/05/24. It has been decided to extend the duration of this curfew today, 2082/05/24, from 11:00 AM to 6:00 PM within the following designated boundaries. All security agencies are directed to implement this order.

content: Boundaries:

content.content: East: Bypass Road
West: Sirsiya Bridge
South: Shankaracharya Gate
North: Gandak Chowk

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24 (Extension)

Following the curfew issued for Birgunj Metropolitan City from 11:00 AM to 6:00 PM on 2082/05/24, it has been decided to further extend the curfew within the following boundaries until further notice. All security agencies shall ensure the implementation of this order.
Boundaries:

content.content: East: Bypass Road
West: Sirsiya Bridge
North: Gandak Chowk
South: Shankaracharya Gate

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24 (Military Mobilization)

content.content: Reviewed the overall peace and security situation and contemporary security issues. Security arrangements shall be further strengthened through coordinated information exchange among security agencies.

During the 'Gen Z' protests, significant damage occurred to private and public property within Birgunj Metropolitan City due to arson, stone-pelting, and unruly activities. As the situation suggests a high risk of continued unrest, a curfew remains in place. However, as the current situation is difficult for the Nepal Police and Armed Police Force Nepal to control alone, it has been decided to request the mobilization of the Nepalese Army (Shreejung Battalion, Parsa) under Section 6(b)(i) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.

content: Bagmati Province
District Administration Office, Dolakha
Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: In the context of protests by Gen Z youth groups across the country regarding the social media ban, all security agencies shall maintain high alert and conduct close monitoring to prevent any undesirable activities within this district.
The overall peace and security situation of the district was found to be normal.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: Reviewed previous decisions.
Discussed the situation arising after the Gen Z protests in Kathmandu Valley and other locations on 2082/05/23, and its impact on district security.

Given the situation following the Gen Z protests, it is deemed insufficient for the Nepal Police and Armed Police Force alone to prevent unrest at the District Administration Office, other government offices, local municipalities, radio stations, cable cars, and hydropower plants. Therefore, assistance from the Nepalese Army (Shree Garuddal Battalion, Charikot) is requested under Section 6(2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.

If peace cannot be maintained through the use of force at government structures mentioned in Decision No. 3 under Section 6(1)(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, the Chief District Officer's order is sought to use firearms aimed below the knee to restore peace, as per Section 6(1)(b) of the same Act.
The overall peace and security situation of the district was found to be normal.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25

content.content: Analyzing the aftermath of the Gen Z protests on 2082/05/23, a Prohibitory Order (Nishedhagya) is issued for the entire Dolakha district effective from 9:00 AM on 2082/05/24 until further notice, prohibiting any form of assembly, procession, demonstration, meeting, or picketing under Section 6(3a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.
The overall peace and security situation of the district was found to be normal.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25 (Operational)

content.content: To address security challenges following the Gen Z protests and ensure coordinated management and reporting, a Joint Operation Center (JOC) is established as follows:

content: Joint Operation Center (JOC) Composition:

content.content: Captain Mr. Krishna Kaji Thapa, Coordinator, Garuddal Battalion
Administrative Officer Mr. Dil Krishna Shrestha, Member, District Administration Office
Police Inspector Mr. Shriram Timalsina, Member, District Police Office
APF Inspector Mr. Jayanta K.C., Member, Armed Police Force Nepal No. 18 Gulm
Senior Investigation Assistant Mr. Prashant Dotel, Member, National Investigation District Office

Considering reports of prisoners escaping from various jails nationwide, reliable security for the Charikot Jail in Dolakha shall be managed by the Nepalese Army (Garuddal Battalion, Charikot Barracks) and other security units.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25 (Curfew Order)

content.content: Due to the situation following the nationwide Gen Z protests, a curfew is issued effective from 5:00 PM on 2082/05/24 until further notice, under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. Assembly and movement are prohibited within the following boundaries:

content: Curfew Boundaries:

content.content: East: Trindhara on the way to Bhimeshwar Temple
West: Simpani
North: Bhimeshwar Municipality on the way to Pakhatole
South: Old Bazaar

In all other areas of Dolakha district outside the mentioned boundaries, a Prohibitory Order (Nishedhagya) remains in effect until further notice, prohibiting any form of assembly, procession, or meeting under Section 6(3a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.

content: District Administration Office, Dhading
Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: The implementation of previous District Security Committee decisions is proceeding effectively, and the overall situation in the district is normal.

Reviewed the district's status in light of today's nationwide protests. As of 20:00 PM, the situation is normal except for candle lighting at Dharke (Prithvi Highway) and Emale Chowk (Dhadingbesi). As protests are possible within the district, all security agencies shall strengthen security arrangements and information exchange.
Considering security sensitivities, the following security personnel shall be deployed to ensure the protection of government offices and key locations.

content: Boundaries (Cordons):

content.content: Cordon 1: The road between the Livestock Service Office and the Chamber of Commerce and Industry. Responsibility: APF Dependent Gulm Dhading, 1 Section.
Cordon 2: Campus Chowk (in front of Dhading Hospital). Responsibility: APF Dependent Gulm Dhading, 1 Section.
Cordon 3: The inner road in front of the Office of Industry and Commerce. Responsibility: District Police Office Dhading, 7 personnel under the command of a Junior Police Officer.

Cordon 4: The inner road between the Armed Forest Guard Security Camp and Tundikhel (for the security of the helipad as well). Responsibility: District Police Office Dhading, 7 personnel under the command of a Junior Police Officer.

Regular patrolling of the aforementioned designated Cordons shall be conducted by the Armed Police Force, while patrolling of sensitive areas other than the designated Cordons shall be conducted by the Nepal Police.

In the event of an adverse situation regarding peace and security, the Nepalese Army (Shree Bhairav Battalion) shall be mobilized as per the orders of the Chief District Officer in accordance with the Local Administration Act, 1971.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: Since 2082/05/23, protests by the 'Gen Z' group in Kathmandu Valley and various cities across the country have resulted in loss of life and property. As the same group conducted activities such as peaceful protests and candle lighting in this district today, a reliable security arrangement shall be made through mutual coordination among security agencies, focusing on potential future protests or demonstrations.

Protests by the Gen Z group in the district headquarters Dhadingbesi and various market areas along the Prithvi Highway, including chanting slogans, have remained peaceful thus far (19:30 PM) without any loss of life or property.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24 (Emergency Measures)

Prisoners and detainees at the Dhading District Jail (Male Wing) have demanded immediate release and have been observed attempting arson and vandalism inside the facility. It has been found that prisoners whose permanent homes are in this district have been calling relatives in Dhadingbesi, requesting to be freed through jailbreaks similar to incidents at Nakkhu Jail. Additionally, some youths have been seen moving around the market area with sharp weapons like khukuris and swords. Assessing this critical situation, the Dhadingbesi area of Neelakantha Municipality Ward No. 3 (bounded by Siran Bazar Chowk to Creek Resort in the East, Hospital Chowk in the West, the road to District Administration in the North, and Arun Khola in the South) is declared a Riot-Affected Area per Section 6(b)(1) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. The Nepalese Army (Shree Bhairav Battalion, Gajuri Barracks) is requested to take action per Section 6(b)(1)(i) of the same act. While a 2:10 PM meeting previously decided to start a curfew at 5:00 PM, considering the security sensitivity of the riot-affected area, a curfew is now ordered from 2:20 PM to 5:00 PM under Section 6(a)(1) of the same act. Security agencies are directed to take action per Sections 6(a)(3) and (4).
Decisions of 2082/05/26

content.content: Discussed and reviewed the incidents in the district resulting from the situation created by Gen Z protests in Kathmandu and other cities on 2082/05/23 and 24. The situation in the district is currently moving toward normalcy.

In the context of the country's adverse situation and the ongoing challenge of undesirable activities at night, a curfew order is issued per Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. Movement, assembly, processions, demonstrations, mass meetings, or picketing are prohibited in the following areas of Neelakantha Municipality, Dhading, from 7:00 PM on 2082/05/26 until 6:00 AM on 2082/05/27.

content: Boundaries:
District headquarters Dhadingbesi (Neelakantha Municipality): East: Izara Chautara; West: Dhadingbesi-Salyantar Road; North: Thopal Khola; South: Arun Khola.

content.content: Due to potential threats to prison security and overall peace in the district following the Gen Z protests on 2082/05/23 and 24, a curfew was in effect from 5:00 PM on 2082/05/24. As the situation is gradually normalizing, for the convenience of the general public, the curfew is lifted from 5:00 PM to 7:00 PM today (2082/05/26). However, a Prohibitory Order (Nishedhagya) shall remain in effect district-wide during this period.

During the period of the prohibitory order, essential goods such as milk, fruits, vegetables, eggs, meat products, agricultural produce, and medicines shall be allowed uninterrupted transport and movement in coordination with security agencies.

content: District Administration Office, Kathmandu
Decisions of 2082/05/22

content.content: Reviewed the overall peace and security situation of Kathmandu district. The situation is currently normal; security arrangements shall be kept efficient and coordinated in the coming days.

Discussed sensitive activities that may be conducted by various individuals or organizations in Kathmandu on 2082/05/23. Considering security sensitivity, potential activities shall be closely monitored to prevent any undesirable actions through coordinated security arrangements.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: To prevent today's demonstrations by the Gen Z group and other parties from turning violent, an integrated security plan approved by the District Security Committee meeting on 2082/05/22 is in place. All officers and personnel on duty shall be briefed on the effective implementation of this security plan.
As there may be significant presence of children in today's demonstrations organized by the Gen Z group, extreme caution must be exercised if force is required for crowd control.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/23 (Follow-up)

content.content: Reviewed the overall peace and security situation of Kathmandu district. While the situation is currently normal, in light of potential sensitive activities by various youth groups regarding the social media ban, security coordination shall be strengthened to ensure error-free security management and prevent undesirable incidents.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/23 (Curfew Recommendation)

content.content: Considering the chaotic situation including confrontations, clashes, and riots involving protesters from various youth groups regarding the social media ban on 2082/05/23, it is recommended to the Chief District Officer to issue a curfew order per Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. This shall be effective from 12:30 PM to 10:00 PM on 2082/05/23 in the following areas.

a) Baneshwor Area:

From Baneshwor Chowk north via Eyeplex Mall up to Ratna Rajya Secondary School,
From Baneshwor Chowk south via Shankhamul slope up to Shankhamul Bridge.

As unruly activities during protests in the Baneshwor area on 2082/05/23 have disrupted peace and security, a request is made to the Nepalese Army (Narasingh Dal Battalion, Singha Durbar Barracks) to provide necessary assistance in implementing the order issued by the Chief District Officer per Section 6(2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.

content: District Administration Office, Lalitpur
Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: As various groups and parties held demonstrations in the Valley area on 2082/05/23, which could lead to violent activities, riots, or unrest and affect public peace and security, a request is made to Lalitpur Metropolitan City, Mahalaxmi Municipality, and Godawari Municipality to close all schools/colleges and educational institutions for 2 (two) days on Bhadra 24 and 25, in accordance with the Local Administration Act, 1971. Additionally, the District Administration Office shall publish a notice to this effect.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/23 (Security Assessment)

content.content: Information has been received from various sources that groups including Gen-Z and other youths are preparing to hold demonstrations in Kathmandu Valley from 2082 Bhadra 23. Therefore, all security agencies shall assess potential security risks, make necessary preparations for reliable security arrangements, and exchange information regularly.

In view of the demonstrations and to protect public property and lives, special alertness shall be maintained in risk-prone areas (VA/VP), including the Ministers' Residence in Bhaisepati, Judges' Residence in Pulchowk, Nepali Congress Central Office, CPN (UML) Central Office, Balkumari Bridge, telecommunication service providers/infrastructure, and other important installations and government offices. Security agencies shall deploy personnel coordinately based on an integrated security plan.
Reviewed the progress of implementation of previous District Security Committee decisions. The peace and security situation of the district has remained normal thus far.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/23 (Prohibited Areas)

content.content: As demonstrations by various groups on 2082/05/23 may lead to violence or unrest, it is recommended to declare the following areas as prohibited zones:

East: The main road leading to Inarmode,
West: National Records Office (Civil),
North: Inland Revenue Office,
South: National Human Rights Commission and National Cooperative Development Board premises.
The area of the new Ministers' Residence in Bhaisepati:
East: The road from Bhaisepati to Bungamati,
West: CG Housing,
North: Bhaisepati Residential Gate,
South: The descent leading to Itani.
The area of the Nepali Congress Central Office in Sanepa:

West: Bagmati Corridor road from Balkhu Bridge to Manmohan Park,
North: Service lane of the Ring Road (from Balkhu Bridge to Sanepa Chowk),
South: The paved road from Indrayani Temple to the Bagmati Corridor.
The area of the CPN (UML) Central Office in Chyasal:

West: Chyasal Stadium,
North: Manohara River,
South: The road leading to Mal Pokhari.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: Due to the potential for violence and unrest arising from demonstrations in the Valley on 2082/05/23, it is recommended to declare the following areas as prohibited zones:

From Mahalaxmi Chowk to Patan Hospital,
From Patan Hospital to Jawalakhel Chowk,
From Jawalakhel Chowk to Ekantakuna,
From Ekantakuna to Mahalaxmi Chowk.

It is recommended to issue a curfew order effective from 9:00 AM on 2082/05/24 until further notice, under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. Movement, assembly, processions, demonstrations, mass meetings, or picketing are prohibited within the following boundaries of Lalitpur Metropolitan City, Lalitpur district.

content: Curfew Boundary:
The entire area inside and including the Ring Road of Lalitpur Metropolitan City, from Balkhu, Dhobighat, Sanepa, Ekantakuna, Mahalaxmisthan, Satdobato, and Gwarko to Balkumari Bridge.
Decisions of 2082/05/24 (Specific Wards)

content.content: Due to potential violent activities and unrest from demonstrations on 2082/05/23, it is recommended to issue a curfew order within the specified four boundaries.

A curfew order shall be issued from 7:00 AM to 12:00 midnight on 2082/05/24, under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, prohibiting movement, assembly, processions, or meetings within the following boundaries of Wards 2, 4, 9, 18, and 25 of Lalitpur Metropolitan City.

South: The descent area leading to Itani.

During the curfew period as per Decision No. 1, the Nepalese Army, Nepal Police, Armed Police Force, and National Investigation personnel shall be deployed in coordination with each other to manage security.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25

content.content: Prepare a factual report on the damages caused to all police divisions, circles, posts, and other units in the district, as well as the Nakkhu Jail Office, during the demonstrations by various groups in the Valley region on 2082/05/23 and 24. Restore security services and resume operations as soon as possible.

Security agencies shall be deployed at Nakkhu Jail, Lalitpur, starting today as per previous arrangements to manage the security of the prison area. The Jail Administrator shall coordinate the immediate placement of prisoners in the CRC block.

content: District Administration Office, Chitwan
Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: It was decided to welcome and offer best wishes for a successful tenure to the Chief District Officer, Mr. Ganesh Aryal, who has joined as the Chief District Officer and Chairman of the District Security Committee.
Reviewed the implementation of decisions from the previous meeting. The overall peace and security situation of the district was found to be normal.

Collect and analyze information regarding the protest programs reportedly organized by the 'Gen-Z' group. Security agencies in the district shall deploy efficient and agile teams to maintain high alertness and ensure error-free peace and security through coordination.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/23 (Follow-up)

content.content: Thank all security personnel deployed in the field for the caution and restraint exercised during the control of demonstrations held at various locations in the district today.
Make security arrangements for Bharatpur Metropolitan City and other sensitive locations during the current adverse situation.
Conduct a comprehensive analysis of the evolving situation and adopt security strategies accordingly.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

In view of recent developments and to prevent further deterioration of peace and security, it was decided to issue a curfew order effective from 12:00 PM on 2082/05/24 until further notice. Per Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, movement, assembly, processions, demonstrations, mass meetings, or picketing are prohibited within the following four boundaries of Bharatpur Metropolitan City, Chitwan.

Decisions of 2082/05/25

It was decided to issue a curfew order from 17:00 PM on 2082/05/25 until 06:00 AM on 2082/05/26 throughout the borders of Chitwan district. Per Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, movement, assembly, processions, demonstrations, mass meetings, or picketing are prohibited.
District Administration Office, Kavrepalanchok
Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: Protests by the 'Gen Z' group in Kathmandu today have disrupted peace and security, creating a situation where a curfew had to be imposed. Considering the possibility that the influence of this movement may reach this district, information regarding the activities of such groups shall be collected and potential risks analyzed. Necessary security personnel from the District Police Office and APF Nepal No. 15 Battalion HQ shall be deployed based on the analysis of the movement's potential nature.

Regarding the request for drone flight permission in the Banepa Municipality area for research titled 'Aboveground Biomass and Carbon Stock Estimation Using LIDAR and Multi-spectral Derived Data' by the Kathmandu University School of Engineering, approval is granted on the condition that security sensitivity, personal privacy, and designated terms are maintained in coordination with security agencies.

In honor of the sacrificial contribution of the late APF Constable Om Bahadur Shah, who attained martyrdom on 2059/10/02 in Krishadevi while performing his duties for the country and the organization, a memorial plaque shall be established at the Chairamahadev premises in Panauti Municipality Ward No. 2.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: The peace and security situation of the district has remained normal and peaceful thus far.

As developments in Kathmandu Valley related to Gen Z's protest call have directly impacted this district, leading to spontaneous protests and condolence meetings, a Command Post was established in the District Security Committee on 2082/05/23 to analyze the situation and deploy security personnel with high alertness.
Members of the District Security Committee remain strategically deployed, continuously monitoring and analyzing the peace and security situation.

In view of the current situation, the Shree Shree Mehar Battalion stationed in Dhulikhel is requested to remain on standby. Relevant agencies are requested to ensure essential services continue while remaining ready for immediate deployment as needed.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24 (Curfew Order)

Due to protests occurring in Kathmandu Valley and other parts of the country following Gen Z's movement on 2082/05/23, and the resulting protests in locations like Banepa, Panauti, Dhulikhel, and Panchkhal where crowds may disrupt public peace and order or lead to violent incidents, it was decided to issue a curfew order per Section 6(1)(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, within the following boundaries and times in Kavrepalanchok district:

content.content: The area within 500 meters on both sides of the Arniko Highway from Sanga Bhanjyang to Dhulikhel Bus Park.
The area within 500 meters on both sides of the Banepa-Nala road from Nala Gumba to Nepal Electricity Authority Chowk, Red Cross Chowk, Scheer Memorial Hospital, and Chandeshwori Temple.
The area within 500 meters on both sides of the road from Banepa Chordobato to Panauti Bazar (Roshi River).
The area within 500 meters on both sides of the B.P. Highway from Kavrebhanjyang to Dhulikhel Chowk, and from Saraswati Bazar/Adrawajar to Bhattedanda.
The area within 500 meters on both sides of the Arniko Highway from Tamaghat Bridge to Zerokilo in Panchkhal Municipality.

content: Effective Date: 2082/05/24 | Time: 12:15 PM to 9:00 PM

content.content: Request the Nepalese Army, Shree Shree Mehar Battalion Dhulikhel, to implement Decision No. 1.
The curfew order issued today (2082/05/24) shall continue until 10:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/25).
Request the Nepalese Army, Shree Shree Mehar Battalion Dhulikhel, to implement Decision No. 1.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24 (District-wide)
The curfew order issued today (2082/05/24) shall be extended to cover the entire Kavrepalanchok district until further notice.
Decisions of 2082/05/24 (Unit Management)
Analyze the manpower, weaponry, and ammunition status of various Nepal Police and APF units in the district and merge/deploy them to appropriate locations as needed based on the security situation.
Decisions of 2082/05/25

content.content: Based on today's analysis following the decisions of 2082/05/24, the overall security situation of the district is considered normal.

Certain prisoners at Dhulikhel Jail attempted to start a fire inside the prison, but the situation was brought under control following security action. As it is appropriate to transfer the prisoners directly involved in the incident to other prisons for security reasons, it was decided to keep them safely in the detention cells of the District Police Office until then.

content: Involved Individuals:

content.content: Ram Krishna Mainali (alias Sagar Mainali)
Uddhavraj Giri
Sunil Ranjitkar
Shivraj Thapa

content: Gandaki Province
District Administration Office, Kaski
Decisions of 2082/05/22

content.content: Reviewed the implementation of previous District Security Committee decisions. The decisions were found to be fully implemented.

It has been understood that groups including Gen Z, young students, and young entrepreneurs in Pokhara plan to hold protests on 2082/05/23 against the government's ban on social media platforms not registered in Nepal. District security agencies shall coordinately collect, analyze, and exchange information to bring undesirable and illegal activities to justice. Additionally, District Police Office Kaski and APF Nepal No. 24 Battalion HQ Kalika, Malepatan, shall jointly ensure error-free security management.

As various organizations, political parties, and groups have submitted memorandums regarding the impact of the social media ban on freedom of expression and the right to information, information on their potential protest programs shall be collected and analyzed to coordinate peace and security.

Regarding the District Police Office Kaski's request (dated 2082/05/18) to upgrade the position ranking of the Pokhara International Airport security office, a request shall be made to the Pokhara International Airport Civil Aviation Office to provide an opinion justifying the need for additional structures and positions.
Prepare a security plan for the upcoming Dashain, Deepawali, and Chhath festivals and present it at the next Security Committee meeting.
Ensure that programs by various organizations, businesses, or political parties obtain permission from local levels; security arrangements shall be made only after such consent is received.
Analysis shows the overall peace and security situation of the district is normal.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: Reviewed the implementation of previous District Security Committee decisions. They were found to be fully implemented.

Considering the possibility that protests by Gen Z, students, and entrepreneurs against the social media ban could turn violent and damage public property, a prohibitory order (Nishedhagya) is issued starting from 2:00 PM on 2082/05/23. Per Section 6, Sub-section (3a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, this applies to the following areas within Pokhara Metropolitan City: Y-Chowk (East), Milan Chowk (North-East), Baidam Fishtail Gate (West), Land Revenue Office Kaski (North), and Rashtra Bank Chowk/Tourism Board Office Damside area (South).

Coordinate with all government offices to deploy joint security personnel to prevent damage to sensitive government residences, public property, and vehicles. Unruly groups engaging in violent or destructive acts contrary to law shall be detained and prosecuted.
Analysis shows the overall peace and security situation of the district is normal.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: Since protests by the Gen Z group in Kaski district from 2082/05/23 have become chaotic and moved beyond the control of local administration and police, a request is made to the local Nepalese Army to mobilize under Section 6, Sub-section (2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, to protect lives and property and maintain peace and order through the implementation of a curfew order.

content: District Administration Office, Syangja
Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: Discussed and reviewed the implementation of previous security decisions and the overall peace and security of the district, which was found to be normal.

While the current situation in the district remains normal despite national developments, all security agencies shall remain on standby, exchanging information and performing minute analysis to prepare for any adverse circumstances that may arise in the coming days.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25

content.content: The impact of the nationwide protest on 2082/05/23 was observed to be moderate in this district. Protesting groups caused minor damage by vandalizing the District Administration Office Syangja, the Putalibazar Municipal Office, the district party offices of the Nepali Congress and CPN-UML, and the statue of B.P. Koirala at Traffic Chowk. Security arrangements have been strengthened to maintain peace and order. In consideration of the current national situation and the influence of the movement in this district, a curfew order is issued throughout Syangja district from 12:00 PM on 2082/05/25 until 6:00 AM on 2082/05/26, in accordance with Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971 (including amendments), to protect the lives and property of citizens.

Given the recently developed scenarios threatening peace and security, a Joint Operation Cell comprising all security agencies (Nepalese Army, Nepal Police, and Armed Police Force) shall be established and operationalized to monitor sensitive locations and areas and ensure immediate deployment when necessary.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25 (Follow-up)

content.content: Based on the monitoring and analysis of the prohibitory order status by all security agencies until 17:00 PM today, which found effective deployment, a curfew order is issued throughout Syangja district from 17:00 PM today (2082/05/25) until 06:00 AM tomorrow (2082/05/26), in accordance with Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971 (including amendments).
A Joint Operation Center (JOC) shall be established to collect and analyze information regarding the current peace and security arrangements in the district, consisting of the following officials:

content: JOC Composition:

content.content: Administrative Officer Mr. Surya Prasad Sharma, District Administration Office Syangja - Coordinator
Captain Mr. Kalyan Thapa, Singh Shardul Gulm, Aandhikhola Barracks, Syangja - Member
Police Inspector Mr. Kamal Nepali, District Police Office Syangja - Member
Investigation Officer Mr. Sushil Koirala, National Investigation District Office Syangja - Member
APF Inspector Mr. Devraj Malla, APF Nepal No. 30 Gulm HQ, Syangja - Member

content: District Administration Office, Parbat
Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: Reviewed the implementation of the decisions from the previous meeting. The status of implementation was found to be satisfactory.

As the impact of the Gen Z movement occurring in various parts of the country since 2082/05/23 has also been observed in this district, it was decided that all security agencies will closely monitor the diverse activities of said group, transmit information timely, and adopt alertness and caution based on the information received.
It was decided to direct the heads of government offices in the district to operate with special alertness according to the changing circumstances.
It was decided to approve and implement the Sectoral Special Security Plan 2082 for the fiscal year 2082/083 for this district.
The overall situation of the district was found to be peaceful.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24 (Additional)

content.content: Due to the current movement of Gen Z in most parts of the country and the resulting circumstances, it is deemed necessary to conduct joint deployment including Nepal Police and Armed Police under the leadership of the Nepalese Army to normalize the situation and ensure the safety of citizens' lives and property. Therefore, it was decided to mobilize joint security forces using powers under Section 6, Sub-section (3a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.

District Administration Office, Rupandehi
Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: In view of the potential for demonstrations, processions, riots, and clashes in the Bhairahawa and Butwal areas on 2082/05/23, which could create an adverse situation and breach peace and security, a curfew order is issued for sensitive areas within the Butwal to Bhairahawa corridor.

During the curfew period, all security agencies shall coordinate and collaborate to control prohibited activities within the designated boundaries and enhance security management. If necessary, a request shall be made to mobilize the Nepalese Army.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: The overall peace and security situation of the district was discussed and analyzed.
The security situation thus far was found to be under control and maintained with high alertness.

Considering the possibility of demonstrations, processions, riots, and clashes in various parts of Rupandehi district that could disturb peace and security, a prohibitory order (Nishedhagya) is issued throughout the district starting from 9:30 AM on 2082/05/24 until further notice, in accordance with Section 6, Sub-section 3(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971 (including amendments).

All security agencies shall coordinate and collaborate to control prohibited activities during the prohibitory order and enhance security management. If required, the Nepalese Army shall be requested for assistance.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: In view of the adverse situation created by demonstrations, processions, riots, and clashes throughout Rupandehi district on 2082/05/24, which could lead to a breach of peace and security, a curfew order is issued until further notice.
To control prohibited activities within the specified boundaries during the curfew period, all security agencies shall coordinate and collaborate to make security management more effective.
A request shall be made to mobilize the Nepalese Army during the said curfew period.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/27

content.content: The overall peace and security situation of the district was discussed and analyzed. The situation thus far was found to be under control and maintained with high alertness.

In view of potential demonstrations, processions, riots, and clashes in various areas of Rupandehi district that could create adverse conditions and breach peace and security, a prohibitory order (Nishedhagya) is issued effective from 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM, and a curfew order from 10:00 PM to 6:00 AM, throughout Rupandehi district starting from 2082/05/27 until further notice, in accordance with Section 6, Sub-section 3(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971 (with amendments).
During the period of the prohibitory order, all security agencies shall coordinate and collaborate to control prohibited activities and enhance the effectiveness of security management.
A request shall be made to mobilize the Nepalese Army during the specified period.

content: District Administration Office, Banke
Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: Considering the sensitive current situation where demonstrations are being held under the name of 'Gen-Z' in Kathmandu and various parts of the country, and in light of today's demonstrations in Nepalgunj, Kohalpur, and other locations within this district, all security agencies shall remain in a state of full alertness. They shall analyze potential security challenges and deploy coordinately through effective intelligence, patrolling, and public announcements (miking) to maintain normalcy and exchange reliable information.

Special priority shall be given to keeping the upcoming major festivals of Hindu devotees safe, peaceful, and harmonious. Immediate legal action shall be ensured against individuals who take the law into their own hands or attempt to engage in undesirable activities.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

On 2082/05/24, following demonstrations by groups involving various individuals after approximately 9 AM, certain unruly groups in the Nepalgunj market area and surrounding urban settlements of Banke district engaged in destructive acts, including arson and looting of Nepalgunj Sub-Metropolitan City, Kohalpur Municipality, District Administration Office, Land Revenue, Survey, Customs, Inland Revenue offices, High Court, District Court, and numerous other government offices, political party offices, and private residences. Furthermore, security posts and personnel have been attacked. Given this adverse situation and the high probability of chaos, violence, clashes, and further destruction of public structures and government offices, it is deemed essential to protect the lives and property of the general public and maintain peace, tolerance, and communal harmony. Therefore, it was decided to recommend to the Chief District Officer to issue a prohibitory order (Nishedhagya) effective from 10 PM tonight until further notice, in accordance with the Local Administration Act, 1971, in the following areas:

content.content: East: Rapti River Bridge
West: Via Khajura Road to the Canal Bridge
North: Ranjha Chowk
South: Jamunaha Border Point
The area up to 500 meters on both sides of the East-West Highway within the district boundaries.

content: To maintain peace and order and control chaotic activities like looting and arson, all security agencies in the district shall be jointly mobilized.
District Administration Office, Kapilvastu
Decisions of 2082/05/20

content.content: Reviewed the status of implementation of the decision made on 2082 Bhadra 23.
Approved the draft of the 'Sectoral Special Security Plan, 2082,' prepared in view of security sensitivity per the District Security Committee Kapilvastu's decision on 2082/04/28.

On the occasion of the birth anniversary of Prophet Muhammad (Mohammad Day) on 2082/05/20, as a large number of Muslim devotees will gather in Taulihawa, Krishnanagar, Chandrauta, and other locations to organize religious rallies and programs, security agencies shall arrange reliable security management considering potential security challenges.
Analyzed the overall peace and security situation of the district as normal.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: Reviewed the status of implementation of the decision made on 2082 Bhadra 20.

As the Gen-Z demonstrations occurring in Kathmandu and various parts of the country may impact this district, all security agencies shall immediately analyze the district's situation and potential security challenges to strengthen security arrangements.
Analyzed the overall peace and security situation of the district as normal.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25

content.content: Reviewed the status of implementation of the decision made on 2082 Bhadra 23.

Despite the announcement that the movement called by Gen-Z has ended, certain unruly groups are attempting to engage in vandalism of public and private property, arson, and looting in various parts of the district. Therefore, it was decided to issue a curfew order throughout the district from 5:00 PM on 2082/05/25 to 6:00 AM on 2082/05/26. A request shall be made to the Shamsher Dal, Gorusange, Kapilvastu, to mobilize the Nepalese Army during this period.
Analyzed the overall peace and security situation of the district as normal.

content: District Administration Office, Dang
Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: Reviewed the implementation status of the decisions made by the District Security Committee meeting on 2082/05/14.

Following reports that some groups have engaged in protests and vandalism resulting in loss of life and property in various districts under the pretext that the government closed social media, and given that protests have also occurred in this district, all security agencies shall be deployed coordinately to prevent uncontrolled demonstrations and unwanted activities like vandalism, arson, and looting of public property, government offices, and private assets. Reliable and error-free security management shall be ensured with high alertness.

In view of the possibility of protesters moving from one location to another or coming from other districts to the provincial capital and major market areas, checks at main entry points and roads of the district shall be tightened, and the movement of suspicious groups shall be restricted.

To make district peace and security effective, the District Police Office Dang and APF Nepal No. 29 Battalion HQ Bhalubang were assigned to draft an Integrated Security Sectoral Action Plan in coordination with all agencies. The plan, incorporating suggestions discussed in the meeting, was approved and shall be implemented.
The peace and security situation of the district thus far was found to be normal.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: Reviewed the implementation status of the decisions made by the District Security Committee meeting held on 2082/05/23.

In light of the tragic and unimaginable incidents involving loss of life and property during protests and vandalism in districts including Kathmandu, and as protests and processions are occurring in various locations within this district, there is a risk of such crowds becoming uncontrolled, violent, and destructive, leading to arson and vandalism of public property, government offices, and private assets. Therefore, it was decided to recommend to the Chief District Officer to issue a curfew order effective from 4:30 PM on 2082/05/24 until further notice, in accordance with Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, within the following four boundaries of Dang district, prohibiting movement, assembly, processions, demonstrations, mass meetings, or picketing.

content: Boundaries:

content.content: Within Rapti Rural Municipality (Provincial Assembly, Office of the Chief Minister and Council of Ministers area) and Bhalubang Bazar area: Gopi Nala to the West, Bhalubang Bridge to the East.

Within Ghorahi Sub-Metropolitan City area: Jhigoura Chowk to the West; Katuwa Khola Bridge near Gorkha Boarding School from the Police Boarding School to the South; Miteri Hospital to Razhena School to the East; and Phursekali/Dhoreni School to Sewar Khola Bridge to the North.
Within Tulsipur Sub-Metropolitan City area: Hatti Khauwa to the East, Krishna Mandir to the West, Eye Hospital to the North, and Airport to the South.
Within Lamahi Municipality area: Arnawa to the East, Arjun Khola Bridge to the West, and Kolahi Bridge to the South.
It was decided that security agencies would arrange for public announcements (miking) to ensure the general public is informed of the curfew in the mentioned market areas.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25

content.content: Reviewed the implementation status of the decisions made by the District Security Committee meeting held on 2082/05/24.

Following the curfew order issued by the Chief District Officer effective from 4:30 PM on 2082/05/24 until further notice under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, it was decided to provide necessary facilitation for the transportation of essential goods such as food items, medicine, drinking water, petroleum products, dairy products, fruits, and vegetables for the general public during the curfew period.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25 (Follow-up)

content.content: In accordance with today's decision of the Dang District Security Committee dated 2082/05/25, the curfewwhich was effective from 4:30 PM on 2082/05/24 until 6:00 AM on 2082/05/26will be extended again from 9:00 AM on 2082/05/26 until midnight (12:00 AM). It was decided to recommend to the Chief District Officer to issue this curfew order under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, within the specified boundaries, prohibiting movement, assembly, processions, demonstrations, mass meetings, or picketing.

content: Boundaries (Reconfirmed):

content.content: Within Rapti Rural Municipality (Provincial Assembly and Bhalubang Bazar area): Gopi Nala to the West, Bhalubang Bridge to the East.

Within Ghorahi Sub-Metropolitan City area: Jhigoura Chowk to the West; Police Boarding School to Katuwa Khola Bridge to the South; Miteri Hospital to Razhena School to the East; and Phursekali/Dhoreni School to Sewar Khola Bridge to the North.
Within Tulsipur Sub-Metropolitan City area: Hatti Khauwa to the East, Krishna Mandir to the West, Eye Hospital to the North, and Airport to the South.
Within Lamahi Municipality area: Arnawa to the East, Arjun Khola Bridge to the West, and Kolahi Bridge to the South.
It was decided that security agencies would arrange for public announcements (miking) in the mentioned market areas to inform the general public of the curfew extension.

content: कर्णाली प्रदेश
जिल्ला प्रशासन कार्यालय मुगु

content.content: श्री सडक डिभिजन जुम्लाको प.सं. २०८२।८३, च.नं. ९१, मिति २०८२।०५।१४ गतेको पत्रबाट सडक डिभिजन कार्यालय र निर्माण व्यवसायीबिच ठेक्का सम्झौता भई सडक डिभिजन कार्यालय जुम्लाबाट कर्मचारी खटिई फिल्ड प्रतिवेदन अनुसार उक्त ठेक्काको ५.८४ कि.मि. सडक खण्ड चट्टानमा बाटो खोल्न तथा बाटो चौडा गर्न निर्माण व्यवसायी स्वयमको खर्चबाट मुगु जिल्लाको छायल अगाडिको कडा चट्टानमा बाटो खोल्न तथा बाटो चौडा गर्न एक मात्र विकल्प विस्फोटक पदार्थको प्रयोग रहेको साथै लागत अनुमानमा समेत Hard Rock को Quantity भएको हुँदा Explosive Material को खरिद, ढुवानी, भण्डारण र प्रयोगको अनुमतिको लागि सिफारिस भई आएकोले विस्फोटक पदार्थको खरिद, ओसारपोसार, सञ्चय र प्रयोगको इजाजत प्रदान गर्ने सम्बन्धी कार्यविधि, २०७३ बमोजिम गृह मन्त्रालय, लागूऔषध नियन्त्रण शाखालाई सिफारिस गरी पठाउने निर्णय गरियो ।

Reviewed the decisions of the previous meeting. Given the context of nationwide protests by the Gen-Z generation expressing grief against widespread corruption and irregularities in every sector of Nepal, protests occurred today in Gamgadhi, the headquarters of Mugu district. To prevent unpleasant incidents that could disturb peace and security, patrols have been mobilized from the District Police Office and Armed Police Force Nepal No. 45 Gulm HQ Mugu. All security agencies will remain alert, exchanging information, coordinating, and collaborating to ensure the protests do not turn violent.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: Detailed discussions were held regarding the impact on this district resulting from the loss of life and property caused by the nationwide protest movement regarding corruption and irregularities led by Generation Z since 2082/05/23. To control potential negative or wrong steps in the district arising from these protests, the District Security Committee will fully prepare to exchange information among all security agencies, conduct minute situational analysis, deploy joint patrols, and mobilize the Local Administration Act, 1971, and ministry circulars if an adverse situation arises.

To provide quick and accessible emergency health services for potential casualties or injuries resulting from arrests or clashes during this adverse situation, the Health Service Office and other concerned stakeholders shall keep necessary vehicles (available ambulances and others) and skilled manpower in a state of readiness.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25

content.content: To minimize the loss of life and property that may occur in this district due to the nationwide protest movement regarding corruption and irregularities started by Generation Z on 2082/05/23, and to ensure continuous monitoring, inspection, and information exchange, a Joint Operations Center (JOC) shall be formed at the District Administration Office under the leadership of Chief District Officer Mr. Khadananda Khatri, consisting of heads of all security agencies as members.

In view of the existing adverse situation, and to maintain peace and order while controlling incidents such as vandalism, arson, and looting of private and public property in the name of the movement, and to skillfully manage other potential threats, the public shall be notified not to gather in large numbers in public places from 17:00 PM on 2082/05/24 to 06:00 AM on 2082/05/26 throughout the district.

The Special Sectoral Security Action Plan 2082 was passed, covering crime investigation, curfew, festival security, crowd control, highway security, drug control, disaster management, and headquarters security plans.

content: Sudurpashchim Province
District Administration Office, Kailali
Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: Reviewed the implementation of previous Security Committee decisions. The analysis of the overall peace and security situation of the district was found to be normal and satisfactory.

In view of the latest developments in the country and potential security challenges arising from the demonstrations and protest programs by the Gen Z group, all security agencies shall be deployed coordinately to make reliable security arrangements.

Discussed the letter from Seti Provincial Hospital (Ref No. 3545) dated 2082/05/22. In that regard, necessary coordination will be maintained with the Department/Office of Prison Management; the management of most injured prisoners has reached the final stage, and the remaining few will be managed as soon as possible through coordination. Additionally, necessary support and facilitation will be provided to the hospital in accordance with prevailing laws and policies.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: Reviewed the implementation of previous Security Committee decisions. The overall peace and security situation of the district was analyzed to be normal and satisfactory.

Considering the latest developments in the country and potential circumstances arising from the Gen Z group's demonstrations and protests, a restricted zone shall be declared to control chaotic activities, ensure the safety of citizens' lives and property, and protect government property. If the situation remains uncontrolled even after that, a curfew order shall be issued.

Since the demonstrations and protests by the Gen Z group have created a state of chaos, and it appears a situation under Section 6(2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971 might arise, security agencies are directed to exercise powers under Section 6(b) of the Local Administration Act, 1971 until further notice.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25

content.content: Reviewed the implementation status of decisions made in the previous Security Committee meeting.

Identify the details of damage to government offices in the district that occurred on 2082/05/24, present them to the District Security Committee as soon as possible, and make necessary preparations to resume services.
Establish joint patrolling and checkpoints to guarantee security to the public at the district level.

For displaced police circles, divisions, and temporary police posts that were destroyed and where work cannot be performed, identify suitable nearby locations or arrange for operations to be conducted from the District Police Precinct.

To resume services provided by local levels, Chief Administrative Officers shall submit a report to the District Security Committee mentioning the current situation and additional arrangements required; services shall be resumed in local levels where it is possible to do so.

Appeal to the local community to cooperate from their respective sectors to maintain peace and security and normalize daily life by following the requests made as per the District Security Committee's decisions, and conduct public announcements (miking) for the same.

Considering the latest security situation in the district, the indefinite curfew order issued by the Chief District Officer of Kailali Districtprohibiting movement (in groups of more than three), any kind of assembly, procession, demonstration, mass meeting, meeting, or picketing effective throughout the district from 05:00 PM on 2082/05/24shall continue until further notice, and all security agencies shall further coordinate for its effective implementation.

content: District Administration Office, Kanchanpur
Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: Reviewed the implementation of decisions from the previous District Security Committee meeting. Implementation of said decisions was found to be effective.

Regarding social media regulation and control, being highly sensitive toward the incidents of 2082 Bhadra 23 in Kathmandu Valley and various cities across the country at the call of the Gen Z group, all security agencies shall identify potential incident sites through mutual communication to regulate protests, meetings, processions, and other illegal activities, and to prevent loss of life and damage to public property. High alertness shall be maintained through Area Domination, guarding, and patrolling to bring potential incidents under control.
Considering the recent sensitivity of the overall peace and security of the district, a Command Post shall be established under the coordination of the Chief District Officer as follows.

content: Command Post Composition:

content.content: Coordinator - Chief District Officer
Member - Superintendent of Police
Member - Superintendent of Armed Police
Member - Deputy Investigation Director
Member - Major (Army)
Member - Assistant Chief District Officer
Member - Medical Superintendent Mr. Arjun Bhatt

content: Decisions of 2082/05/25

content.content: Reviewed the implementation of decisions from the previous District Security Committee meeting.

Considering the current adverse situation, Nepal Police, Armed Police Force Nepal, and the National Investigation District Office shall be jointly mobilized in mutual coordination with the Nepalese Army to maintain peace, security, and public order and to control unwanted activities.

In reference to the Government of Nepal, Ministry of Defense, Nepalese Army, Directorate of Public Relations and Information, Army HQ Kathmandu's press release no. 14 dated 2082/05/25 regarding 'Surrendering unauthorized weapons and ammunition to security personnel,' a notice shall be published for the information of all concerned to immediately return looted/found weapons, ammunition, uniforms, and security equipment from security personnel during the Gen Z-led movement to the nearest security agency or personnel, and requesting individuals with information regarding such weapons to inform security agencies.
Publish a notice for the prisoners who escaped from the District Jail, Kanchanpur, during the movement on 2082/05/24 to come into contact immediately.
The overall peace and security situation of the district is currently under control.

content: Impact:

content.content: It appears that because weapons and ammunition were looted and prisoners escaped from the District Jail, Kanchanpur, during the movement on 2082/05/24, a curfew order was issued within a specified perimeter on Bhadra 25, prohibiting any kind of assembly, procession, demonstration, mass meeting, meeting, or picketing throughout the district to bring the situation under control.

There appears to have been a lack of prior assessment of the incident; since it was the home district of the Home Minister, the physical damage was even more unimaginable, indicating overall weaknesses in adopting security sensitivity.

content: District Administration Office, Dadeldhura
Decisions of 2082/05/23

content.content: During the review of the implementation of past decisions, the meeting evaluated the status of decision implementation as satisfactory.

Although chaotic activities of the group named 'Gen Z Movement Nepal' have not been observed in this district recently, it was decided to collect information to prevent potential chaotic activities by the said group. All security agencies in the district will maintain high alertness through mutual coordination and arrange reliable security measures. It was decided that the District Police Office, Armed Police Force Nepal No. 52 Gulm HQ Dadeldhura, and the National Investigation District Office Dadeldhura would coordinate to closely monitor the activities of said group and take necessary legal action against anyone involved in unlawful acts.
The meeting held a detailed discussion on the contemporary security situation of the district. It was analyzed that the current peace and security situation in the district remains normal.

content: Decisions of 2082/05/24

content.content: During the review of the implementation of past decisions, the meeting evaluated the status of decision implementation as satisfactory.

In view of recent developments in the country and considering the situation arising from demonstrations and protest programs organized by the Gen Z group, and to control potential chaotic activities, ensure the safety of lives and property of ordinary citizens, and protect government property to maintain peace and order, a curfew order is issued for the following locations due to out-of-control circumstances.

content: Locations:

content.content: Bagbazar area, Tufandanda area of Amargadhi Municipality Ward No. 5, and the area surrounding the District Police Office.
The area from the District Administration Office to Tundikhel in Amargadhi Municipality Ward No. 4.
The area surrounding Kirtipur in Amargadhi Municipality Ward No. 8.
As a situation under Section 6(2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971 (2028 BS) has arisen, a request shall be made to mobilize the Nepalese Army to maintain peace and security.

Since demonstrations and protest programs by the Gen Z group have created a state of chaos and a situation under Section 6(2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971 is likely to occur, security agencies are directed to exercise powers under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971 until further notice.

content: Chapter - 10: Regarding the Collection of Physical Evidence by the Commission
10.1. Details Regarding CCTV, Audio/Video, Media, and Materials Published on Social Media

During the investigation, the Commission corresponded with various agencies to analyze incidents during the demonstrations, requesting CCTV footage and issuing public notices to collect Audio/Video evidence. Furthermore, Audio/Video materials related to the incidents available on social media were also collected. The details are as follows.

grid.content: कोहलपुर नगर प्रमुख पूर्ण आचार्यको निजी निवासका फोटो र भिडियोहरु
भूमि व्यवस्था सहकारी तथा गरिवी निवारण मन्त्रालय

content: Practical Limitations Regarding the Berkeley Protocol

During the investigation conducted by this Commission, various types of digital materials have been obtained, including CCTV footage, videos submitted by victims and the general public, and content downloaded from social media. These materials have been received via pen drives, email, and messaging applications.

Due to these practical circumstances, the Commission cannot fully implement some of the technical requirements specified in the 'Berkeley Protocol on Digital Open-Source Investigations™'. A summary of the major limitations and acceptable alternatives is presented below.
1. Full Chain of Custody is Not Feasible
According to the Berkeley Protocol, the following are expected:

content.content: Original files
A controlled collection process with documented evidence
Continuous chain-of-custody
Forensically sound transfer methods

content: However, the materials received by the Commission have already been circulated, copied, downloaded, or forwarded. Therefore:

content.content: The Commission is not the original collector
Pre-handling records of the material are unavailable
There is a high probability that the initial storage device cannot be found
Files may have been altered before submission
It is impossible to reconstruct a high-level chain-of-custody

content: This limitation is natural and inevitable in an investigation of a fact-finding nature.
2. Metadata (EXIF, Time, Device Details) is Lost or Altered
Social media platforms typically:

content.content: Remove or alter EXIF data
Recompress and transcode videos
Erase original device identifiers
Remove GPS and time-related metadata

content: Similarly, files sent via WhatsApp, Messenger, Viber, or email also lose their original metadata.

content.content: The Commission cannot perform authenticity testing based on metadata
Metadata-based validation conducted in forensic laboratories is not practical

content: 3. Difficulty in Ensuring Authenticity of AI-Generated or Manipulated Content
Without the following:

content.content: The original device used to record the video
Primary, uncompressed files
Complete metadata format

content: It is not possible to determine with absolute certainty whether a video seen on social media has been altered using AI tools, Deepfakes, or other editing techniques. The recompression process used by social media platforms causes many forensic markers to be lost.
Therefore, the Commission must rely on content-based analysis, such as:

content.content: Geolocation
Chronolocation
Physical and environmental consistency
Visual comparison
Cross-corroboration

content: These methods align with the Berkeley Protocol.
4. The Berkeley Protocol offers flexibility for open-source and citizen-submitted materials
The Protocol acknowledges that:

content.content: Many investigations rely on secondary or open-source materials
Original devices are often unavailable
There is a high probability of metadata loss
Verification depends more on analysis than on forensic purity

content: Therefore, within the framework of the Protocol, the Commission can focus on the following:

content.content: Verification
Corroboration
Contextual analysis
Transparency regarding limitations

content: 5. Extracting Screenshots from Video is Appropriate and Necessary
To clearly present key events in the report, the Commission is extracting screenshots from the obtained videos. This practice is internationally accepted, especially when:

content.content: It is not possible to include the full video in the report
It is necessary to visually demonstrate key moments of an event

content: Points to clarify:

content.content: Screenshots are not considered original evidence
The original video remains the primary source
Screenshots are used solely for visual reference
The Berkeley Protocol also allows the presentation of keyframes for documentation and verification

content: Using Screenshots helps to:

content.content: Clarify the location, time, and sequence of events
Compare materials from different angles
Make the report readable and clear

content: Conclusion
Given the Commission's operational context, it is not possible to fully implement the following technical aspects of the Berkeley Protocol:

content.content: Forensic chain-of-custody
Metadata-based authenticity testing
Definitive identification of AI-manipulation
Handling of original devices or primary files

content: However, the Commission can fully implement the core aspects of the Protocol:

content.content: Verification of content
Corroboration
Contextual analysis
Transparency regarding limitations
Clear and credible report presentation via screenshots

content: This procedure fully aligns with international fact-finding standards and renders the Commission's findings credible, fair, and legally sound.

A total of 174,420 mobile numbers were obtained from both service providers, NTC and NCELL, as detailed below.
